creator: sax@noart.de
text:
(In reply to Matthias Andree from comment #1)
At ftp://ftp.gnu.org/gnu/gprolog/ you can see that 1.4.5 there is from February 2019.
The comment you refer to as duplicate is dated from 2018.
So there is likely a never version with same reviosion available.

What I simply did is to copy ports/lan gprolog to my home, changed the Makefile to 1.4.5 and commented out the BROKEN and DEPRECEATED flags.

I created a new distinfo file:

SHA256 (gprolog-1.4.5.tar.gz) = bfdcf00e051e0628b4f9af9d6638d4fde6ad793401e58a5619d1cc6105618c7c
SIZE = 3587361


Then I did a "su -" make install.

No errors, everything fine. Then I used the gplc to compile a prolog file. No errors and works now for 2 days.

That's all.
 Detlef
